translated: What makes you come here and you are still looking for something? You have gotten what you came to look for. What else do you want?

translated: He spent the night there and in the morning he got up to go on his journey. As he was going, he came across a fig tree. He went to it to look for figs but did not find any. He then said to the fig tree, “Let no one eat figs from you again.” And immediately the fig tree withered. The disciples saw it and were amazed. “How did the fig tree wither immediately?” they asked. Jesus answered, “Truly I tell you, if you have faith and do not doubt, not only will you do what has been done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, ‘Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,’ it will be done. Whatever you ask for in prayer with faith, you will receive.”

translated: You have done well to come and tell me this news, and I am glad that you have come to inform me. I was worried about you, I thought something bad had happened to you, but it seems you are now okay.

translated: My mother was going to the farm with her load of food, when she met a strong wind that was blowing, and it swept her food load and carried it far away, she ran after it but it was in vain, she chased it to a big tree. The food load went and got stuck on the tree.

translated: The child shouted to Zezi saying: “You are the one I have been telling my father about, you are the one who spoiled my cloth.”

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: O ma ja lilwe wʋm jɩŋa o vaŋɩ o ja nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm daa. O ma twɩ lileeru o kɩ o yiǝ nɩ, yɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa lilwe wʋm baŋa nɩ. O laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ naɩ woŋo na?»
translated: What is it that you want to eat before you go? I have already prepared food for you, and I have also fried some meat, and I have made some porridge. My mother asked me saying: “What do you want to eat?”
